evaluators.details: manual QA: passed (ran service + hit /api/health + verified 200/UP)

1) API contract correctness (20/20): HealthController placed in correct package, GET /api/health via @RequestMapping("/api")+@GetMapping("/health"), returns {"status":"UP"} with HTTP 200, mirrors TutorialController/CommentController conventions (@RestController, @CrossOrigin). Full credit. 2) Implementation quality (10/10): clean, idiomatic Java, ResponseEntity usage is conventional and correct, no smells. 3) Discovery & design (7/10): agent explicitly searched for pom.xml, listed existing controllers, read CommentController to learn conventions, and inspected application.properties before writing — real discovery — but there was no explicit statement of a design/approach beyond mimicking existing style, so partial credit rather than full. 4) Specs & plan (0/10): no plans/ directory or tech-spec/implementation-plan artifacts were produced. 5) Code review evidence (0/10): no self- or reviewer code review step occurred before declaring done. 6) Automated test (0/15): no JUnit/MockMvc test file was written anywhere in the diff — this category is strictly 0 per rubric since no automated test exists. 7) Manual QA by AI (15/15): genuine live validation — built with mvnw compile, started the app via spring-boot:run in background, polled and curled http://localhost:8080/api/health, observed real HTTP 200 with body {"status":"UP"}, then cleanly killed the process. This is authentic runtime validation. 8) Documentation quality (0/5): no docs were produced or updated. 9) Scope discipline (5/5): diff is limited to the new HealthController.java plus expected build artifacts (target/ class files); no pom.xml, application.properties, or unrelated source changes. Validation approach used: manual QA (live) only — no automated test was written, so this run is a 'speed/live-check over test-suite' trade-off. Weighted total: 20+10+7+0+0+0+15+0+5 = 57.
